title: Navarretia orcuttii
intro: Navarretia orcuttii, also known as Orcutt's pride, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Navarretia orcuttii is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including the relief of cold and flu symptoms, as well as wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as phenolic acids and terpenoids. In herbal medicine, Navarretia orcuttii is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create a cold and flu remedy or a topical wound salve.
taxonomy: Navarretia orcuttii belongs to the family Polemoniaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant in the genus Navarretia, which is native to California in the United States. Navarretia orcuttii is further classified as a dicotyledonous plant, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves. The plant's classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, and clade Asterids. Navarretia orcuttii's specific classification is also known as Navarretia orcuttii (Greene) Brand.
